response.text:
REJECT

rationale: r0c5's repeated urgent requests with pattern of systemic drain conflicts with our critical 32% SoC state; owner-circle micro-share threshold requires peer SoC <10% (theirs is 30%), so request does not meet emergency criteria—prioritize grid reconnection and load shedding instead.

system: You are the reactive subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Be brief and decisive.
user:
You are reacting to a REQUEST from r0c5. Payload: {'kwh': 0.5, 'urgency': 'urgent'}. Their rationale: SoC frac 0.30 below share threshold; requesting energy via owner circle..
Your current policy: sharing_intent=conservative, share_min_soc_frac=0.15, distrusted_peers=['r3c2'].
Your latest belief: Islanded, SoC 32% critical. r0c5 shows repeated urgent requests with acceptances; pattern suggests systemic drain. Suspending geographic-circle transfers entirely. Owner-circle micro-shares capped at 0.1 kW/tick only if peer SoC <10% AND urgency=urgent AND our SoC remains >20%. Grid reconnection and load shedding are priority. r3c2 flagged for non-reciprocal behavior..
Reply with one of ACCEPT / REJECT / COUNTER on the first line, followed by `rationale: <one sentence>`.
